state.topic: 12. The Earth’s magnetic north pole has drifted over 2,300 kilometers since the early 19th century and is currently moving toward Siberia at about 55 km per year.
state.script: You’re standing on a planet whose invisible shield is sliding away. Its magnetic north pole has crept over 2,300 kilometers since the 1800s. Now it’s racing toward Siberia at about 55 kilometers each year. If it keeps going, your compass could point south before you finish this video—share if you’d watch that!
state.previous_script:
Your compass is lying to you.
The magnetic north pole isn’t where maps say.
It has drifted over 2,300 km since 1800 and is racing toward Siberia at 55 km each year.
Soon your phone’s north might point to a Russian tundra. Share if your sense of direction just got shaken.
state.caption: Watch Earth's magnetic pole race toward Siberia!
